title: Judge Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
description: A judge ordered a temporary halt to the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger on Monday, as a state coalition argues it would violate federal antitrust law,
body:
A judge ordered a temporary halt to the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger on Monday, as a state coalition argues it would violate federal antitrust law, leading to higher prices and fewer movies and TV shows.

	Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin granted a 14-day restraining order after hearing argument from both sides on Friday morning. Paramount had previously agreed not to close the transaction before July 22.


	“Plaintiff States’ showing at least demonstrates that serious questions going to the merits remain, weighing in favor of preliminary injunctive relief,” the judge wrote, adding that Paramount has acknowledged it will not be harmed by the delay until the end of September. “Paramount and Warner Bros. will continue to operate as separate, viable companies competing in the marketplace while they wait for the Court to adjudicate this case. The balance of equities, combined with the public’s vital interest in antitrust enforcement, therefore tips sharply in favor of the requested injunctive relief.”
	

	The 12-state coalition, led by California, brought a motion for the temporary restraining order. The states are also seeking a preliminary injunction, which would block the merger until the judge rules on the merits of the states’ lawsuit.

	The 14-day restraining order could be extended to as long as 28 days. Martinez-Olguin also set a hearing on the preliminary injunction for Aug. 3, though that date, too, could be delayed if the parties agree.

	Rob Bonta, the attorney general of California, hailed the judge’s ruling as a “critical first win in our case to ensure this megamerger never sees the light of day.”
	

	“History tells the tale of what happens when a few people have great power over markets that are central to Americans’ lives: fewer opportunities for more people, worse products and services for all people,” Bonta added. “With our lawsuit, we’re fighting for a free and fair market and a thriving film and television industry that serves creatives and audiences alike. We have a full tank of gas, the law on our side, and look forward to continuing to make our case.”

	In antitrust cases, the injunction is often the whole ballgame. If it is not granted, the deal is allowed to close and it becomes almost impossible to unwind later on. But if it is granted, the deal tends to fall apart before the underlying case can go to trial.

	Paramount has pushed for a hearing on the injunction with live witnesses. The company hopes to get a ruling on the injunction by early September. If the deal has not closed by Sept. 30, Paramount will start to owe millions of dollars a day to Warner Bros. investors.

	At the hearing on Friday, Martinez-Olguin telegraphed the outcome of the ruling, suggesting that Paramount had conceded it would not be harmed by a temporary pause. Jeffrey Kessler, arguing for Paramount, offered to stipulate that the transaction would not close for up to 30 days pending a hearing on the injunction motion.

	The states allege that the merger will harm competition in the basic cable and theatrical markets by combining two of the top three cable programmers and two of the top five film distributors. Paramount has pointed to the success of new entrants — A24, Amazon MGM, and others — to argue that the theatrical market is more competitive and dynamic than the state case makes it appear. 

	Paramount also argues that the cable market is in decline, and that the court should thus not rely on the states’ estimates of market concentration.

	Paramount has also argued that the merger is pro-competitive because it will create a stronger rival in the streaming marketplace to dominant players like Netflix and Amazon. But in a footnote, the judge stated that she could not accept the idea that efficiencies in one market offset competitive harms in another.

	“The Court notes separately that it cannot accept Defendants’ argument that the Transaction will produce efficiencies in the streaming market,” she wrote. “Courts have expressly and repeatedly rejected the defense that a challenged merger will result in economic efficiencies ancillary to competition in the relevant market.”

title: ‘Stranger Things,’ ‘Marshals’ Top Final 2025-2026 TV Season Multiplatform Ratings (EXCLUSIVE)
description: The final 35-day multi-platform ratings for the 2025-2026 TV season are finally in, now that we’re more than a month beyond the official end of the
body:
The final 35-day multi-platform ratings for the 2025-2026 TV season are finally in, now that we’re more than a month beyond the official end of the TV season. According to Nielsen’s multiplatform 35-day linear and streaming ratings, Netflix’s “Stranger Things” was still the TV season’s most-watched non-sports series, with 32.9 million viewers, followed by the streamer’s “His & Hers” (25.6 million) and “Sean Combs: The Reckoning” (20.6 million).
	

	But after that, Paramount+’s “Landman,” which was No. 5 in our previous ranker, leapt to No. 4, while CBS’ “Marshals” was the most-watched broadcast show (19.3 million) and Paramount Network’s “Dutton Ranch” was the only cable show to make the ranker (19.2 million). That means Taylor Sheridan held three of the top ten positions this year with multiple series.


	RELATED: 100 Most-Watched TV Series of 2025-26 Across Streaming, Broadcast and Cable: ‘Stranger Things,’ NFL, ‘Tracker,’ ‘Dancing With the Stars’ Top the Charts


	In the broadcast ranker, CBS held four of the top five series including the top two series “Marshals” and “Tracker,” as well as the top-rated comedy “Ghosts.” Four new CBS series also made the chart, making them the top four new series: “Marshals,” “Sheriff Country,” “Boston Blue” and “CIA.” ABC, meanwhile, has the most-watched adults 18-49 series with “High Potential” and most-watched comedy in the demo with “Abbott Elementary.”

	The full 2025-2026 broadcast season took place from Sept. 14, 2025 to May 24, 2026. Here are this year’s final rankers:
	
		TOP 25 BROADCAST/CABLE/STREAMING SHOWS OF 2025-26, TOTAL VIEWERS (35-DAY MULTIPLATFORM)	
RankTitle (Network/SVOD)TOTAL 35- DAY MULTI-PLATFORM VIEWERS (Millions)1.Stranger Things (Netflix)32.92.His & Hers (Netflix)25.63.Sean Combs: The Reckoning (Netflix)20.64.Landman(Paramount+)19.85.Marshals (CBS/ Paramount+)19.36.Dutton Ranch (Paramount Network/ Paramount+)19.27.Bridgerton(Netflix)18.38.Tracker (CBS/ Paramount+)16.39.High Potential (ABC/ Hulu/ Disney+)16.010.The Pitt (HBO Max)13.811.Monster: The Ed Gein Story (Netflix)13.412.Fallout (Prime Video)13.313.The Beast In Me (Netflix)12.714.The Boroughs (Netflix)12.015.The Lincoln Lawyer (Netflix)11.416.Matlock (CBS/ Paramount+)11.217.Sheriff Country (CBS/ Paramount+)10.918.The Rookie (ABC/ Hulu/ Disney+)10.619.Ghosts (CBS/ Paramount+)10.2Will Trent (ABC/ Hulu/ Disney+)10.2The Madison (Paramount+)10.222.NCIS (CBS/ Paramount+)10.023.Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage (CBS/ Paramount+)9.924.Chicago Fire (NBC/ Peacock)9.8Dancing With the Stars (ABC/ Hulu/ Disney+)9.8

	Source: Analysis of Nielsen Multiplatform Data; Big Data P2+ Prime AA, L+35, 9/14-5/24/2026 + Nielsen SCR, 35-Day Total Flight for streaming. Does not include series or specials with less than 4 telecasts.
	
	
		TOP 25 BROADCAST TV SHOWS OF 2025-26, TOTAL VIEWERS (Total Multiplatform 35-Day)	
RankTitle (Network)Total Multiplatform (Millions)1.Marshals (CBS)19.32.Tracker (CBS)16.33.High Potential (ABC)16.04.Matlock (CBS)11.25.Sheriff Country (CBS)10.96.The Rookie (ABC)10.67.Ghosts (CBS)10.2Will Trent (ABC)10.29.NCIS (CBS)10.010.Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage (CBS)9.911.Chicago Fire (NBC)9.8Dancing With the Stars (ABC)9.813.Survivor (CBS)9.714.9-1-1 (ABC)9.415.60 Minutes (CBS)9.316.Chicago Med (NBC)9.2Boston Blue (CBS)9.218.Chicago P.D. (NBC)9.119.Elsbeth (CBS)9.020.FBI (CBS)8.921.Law & Order: SVU (NBC)8.622.Scrubs (ABC)8.523.Fire Country (CBS)8.224.Shifting Gears (ABC)8.1CIA (CBS)8.1

	Source: Analysis of Nielsen Multiplatform Data; Big Data P2+ Prime AA, L+35, 9/14-5/24/2026 + Nielsen SCR, 35-Day Total Flight for streaming.

title: Variety’s Faith & Media Impact Report 2026: Nominations Are Open
description: Variety’s annual Faith & Media Impact Report returns Dec. 2. The list recognizes a thriving segment of the entertainment industry that includes stor
body:
Variety’s annual Faith & Media Impact Report returns Dec. 2. The list recognizes a thriving segment of the entertainment industry that includes storytellers across the religious spectrum who are offering up compelling and creative experiences featuring faith and spirituality for broad audiences.

	Comprised of directors, writers, creators, studio executives and more working in the entertainment industry at large, the Faith and Media Impact Report recognizes those who have made their mark through authentic and accurate coverage and storytelling across major world faiths and spirituality over the past year.
	

	Past Faith & Media Impact Report honorees include Mo Amer & Ramy Youssef; Raphael Bob-Waksberg; Jason Momoa & Thomas Pa’a Sibbett; Hannah Bos & Paul Thureen; Spike Lee & Denzel Washington; Mike Flanagan, Smriti Mundhra, Brad Inglesby and Rian Johnson.


	The 2026 list will be profiled in the Dec. 2 issue of Variety and feted at the third annual Variety Faith and Spirituality in Entertainment Honors presented by the Coalition for Faith and Media.

	In addition, the ceremony will honor individuals who are supporting the frequently underrepresented theme of faith and spirituality in entertainment storytelling with the 2026 Visionary Awards presented by CFAM. These awards celebrate diverse portrayals of faith and spirituality that are broadly compelling and nuanced.

	Past Visionary Award recipients include Viola Davis & Julius Tennon, Bridget Everett, Erica Lipez, Tiffany Haddish, Arian Moayed, Zahn McClarnon, Sheryl Lee Ralph and Jay Shetty.
	

	The deadline for submissions is Oct. 2. Candidates or their respective representatives can find more details and submit their relevant qualifications by clicking on this link.

schema.headline: Variety’s Faith & Media Impact Report 2026: Nominations Are Open

title: Horror-Comedy ‘Bad Karaoke’ Debuts Trailer Ahead of Tubi FrightFest Premiere (EXCLUSIVE)
description:
Horror fans better get ready to stay on pitch … or else!

	“Bad Karaoke,” a new comedy-horror from directors Chris Lightbody and Robert J. Steinmill
body:
Horror fans better get ready to stay on pitch … or else!

	“Bad Karaoke,” a new comedy-horror from directors Chris Lightbody and Robert J. Steinmiller Jr., is set to have its world premiere at Tubi FrightFest, the London-based genre film festival, on Aug. 30 at the ODEON Luxe West End. Lightbody co-wrote the script alongside producer Spencer Yaras.

	Lexi Underwood (“Little Fires Everywhere”), Case Walker (“The Other Two”) and singer Kat Cunning (“Trinkets”) star in the bloody, tongue-in-cheek indie.
	

	Per the official “Bad Karaoke” synopsis, “When wedding band keyboardist Emily discovers an old karaoke machine in the back of a dive bar, she thinks it will lead to a fun-filled night of singing and bonding with her band mates. Unfortunately, this karaoke machine seems to have a thirst for blood. When the singing begins, so does the bloodshed. When an excellent performance allows someone to be spared by the machine, the crew think they’ve figured out how to finally beat it. Proven almost immediately wrong, it takes more horrifying deaths and a visit from a mysterious stranger to realize they may have a chance at survival after all.”

	“We were inspired to make this film after years of painful karaoke performances nearly put us in an early grave,” the directors said in a statement. “We found that many people have had near-death karaoke experiences and we wanted to play with that idea, as well as say something profound about the state of new music. But instead, we made ‘Bad Karaoke.’”

	Watch the trailer below.

schema.headline: Horror-Comedy ‘Bad Karaoke’ Debuts Trailer Ahead of Tubi FrightFest Premiere (EXCLUSIVE)

title: Grupo Mórbido’s Pablo Guisa Boards Two Pics at Costa Rica Media Market, Launches Blood Bank Initiative (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Grupo Mórbido founder/CEO Pablo Guisa has boarded two projects at the Costa Rica Media Market (CRMM) as a producer, a strong endorsement to the revi
body:
Grupo Mórbido founder/CEO Pablo Guisa has boarded two projects at the Costa Rica Media Market (CRMM) as a producer, a strong endorsement to the revived market’s growing status as a genre hub.

	Both projects, “Greetings from Maryland” (“Saludos desde Maryland”) by Ricardo B’atz’ of El Salvador and “What Lives in the Storm” (“Lo que trae la tormenta”) by Miguel Angel Ferrer won the Mórbido Co-Production Award at the market, which means an investment of 10% to 20% percent in the productions’ respective budgets from the company. As a producer, Guisa will be engaged at every phase, from creative development to financing.
	

	Both projects were selected from the Fantastic Lab Central America & Caribbean — the genre film development initiative launched by the Fantastic Pavilion, the Costa Rica Media Market and Grupo Mórbido last year— which in its first edition received 55 submissions from across the region, of which 15 were chosen for intensive development and six finalists announced at Cannes. 


	“Coming from a country where filmmaking is built through perseverance rather than industry, partnering with Pablo Guisa and Mórbido is a unique opportunity. Their experience in horror cinema gives ‘Greetings from Maryland’ the chance to grow beyond its local origins and connect with international audiences. Together, we hope to create a horror film that is both terrifying and deeply human, where supernatural fear becomes a reflection of the real horrors faced by migrant workers in the United States,” said B’atz.

	B’atz, who plans to film in El Salvador and is best known for his social realist debut feature “Hoy,” is delving into genre filmmaking for the first time with “Greetings…” Pic centers on undocumented workers demolishing an abandoned house who unleash a cursed entity that hunts them one by one, transforming the horrors of invisible immigrant labor into supernatural terror.
	

	“We believe in the power of strong creative alliances. Partnering with Pablo Guisa and Grupo Mórbido at this stage in the development of ‘What Lives in the Storm’ is incredibly meaningful. Their vision, experience, and commitment to bold genre storytelling make them the ideal partners as we continue bringing this film to life,” noted Ferrer, who has made several genre shorts and is aiming to shoot “Storm” on the outskirts of Costa Rica’s Caribbean town of Limón.

	A Venezuelan-Cuban-American whose feature “The Shadow of the Sun” represented Venezuela at the 96th Academy Awards. “What Lives in the Storm” follows a reclusive ex-military doctor who shelters a desperate mother and daughter during a fierce Caribbean hurricane, only to find that a horrific creature has also followed them into his home.

	B’atz’ and Ferrer become the first two Latin American directors to join Blood Bank, the new global initiative by Grupo Mórbido and the Fantastic Pavilion, created to “address a structural problem in the global fantastic film industry: The supply is everywhere. The pipeline doesn’t exist,” said Guisa, whose 30+ years in the genre biz has seen him work with the likes of Adrián García Bogliano, Laura Casabe, Cristian Ponce, Edgar Nito, Julio Hernández Cordón, Patricio Valladares and Isaac Ezban.

	“At the Blood Bank, we manage three types of currency: talent, IP and money. Like any bank, we can offer these as separate services to third parties — we can sell talent services, we can sell or license IP, we can finance projects independently. Each of the three pillars can operate on its own for different clients. But we can also work across all three simultaneously — developing original projects with our in-house talent, working with our existing IP library, and producing or co-producing the result. Blood Bank manages assets and builds value across an entire ecosystem — from idea to global release.” Guisa explained.

	“With Mórbido — Fest, TV, Film — Manticora handling distribution, international sales and theatrical release, the Fantastic Pavilion and a presence at every major market in the world, we practically know everyone in the industry. That means we can manage the entire pipeline: from a high concept to pitching at industry events, developing, packaging, co-producing, financing, building marketing strategies, market screenings, festival circuit, theatrical release, streaming, world sales, pay TV, special edition Blu-rays, merchandising and licensing,” he said, adding: “Basically, we can take a project from idea to finished product and get it to audiences worldwide. And now we are doing exactly that — on a global scale.”

schema.headline: Grupo Mórbido’s Pablo Guisa Boards Two Pics at Costa Rica Media Market, Launches Blood Bank Initiative (EXCLUSIVE)

title: 5 Reasons ‘The Odyssey’ Defied Box Office Expectations, Including Imax’s Monster $52 Million Start
description: “The Odyssey” defied the gods odds in its opening weekend, delivering a sensational $124.5 million domestically and $264.1 million globally. After a
body:
“The Odyssey” defied the gods odds in its opening weekend, delivering a sensational $124.5 million domestically and $264.1 million globally. After all, it’s highly unusual for a centuries-old poem that everyone was forced to read in school to be the basis of a popcorn movie.

	Those mighty ticket sales are a testament to the appeal of director Christopher Nolan, who has become a box office brand unto himself through blockbusters like “Oppenheimer,” the “Dark Knight” trilogy, “Inception” and “Interstellar.” With “The Odyssey,” he secured the biggest global debut of his career, surpassing the benchmark set by 2012’s “The Dark Knight Rises” with $249 million, not adjusted for inflation. “The Odyssey” also ranks as the year’s third-largest domestic and worldwide opening following “Toy Story 5” ($159 million domestic, $312 million global) and “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” ($131 million domestic, $372 million global), two sequels in major film franchises.
	

	“This is a Christopher Nolan tour-de-force,” says David A. Gross, who publishes the box office newsletter FranchiseRe. “Having directed 13 films, Nolan has become a unique and singular talent, with a loyal and enthusiastic following.”

	An adaptation of Homer’s 3,000-year-old Greek epic, “The Odyssey” follows Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, who endures a long and treacherous journey home after the Trojan War. Matt Damon leads the starry ensemble, which includes Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Zendaya, Lupita Nyong’o, Robert Pattinson, Charlize Theron, John Leguizamo and Jon Bernthal. With stellar reviews, euphoric word-of-mouth and the full-blown frenzy around premium formats, “The Odyssey” is primed to enjoy an epic run in theaters. (That’s a relief, since Universal spent a staggering $250 million to produce the film.) Now that its grand voyage has begun, Variety unpacks five reasons “The Odyssey” scored in its opening weekend.
	
	
		All In on Imax	

	Sure, Damon, Hathaway and Holland are the stars of “The Odyssey.” But one could argue the real draw is Imax, the technology that’s central to Nolan’s big-screen vision. Because Imax cameras are loud and cumbersome, most movies only utilize the company’s high-tech devices for certain action sequences. “The Odyssey” is significant as the first feature shot entirely with Imax cameras, a feat that Nolan has referred to as his “longest-held ambition.”

	“If you don’t see ‘The Odyssey’ in Imax, you aren’t seeing it in the way that Chris Nolan intended,” the company’s CEO Richard Gelfond told Variety after opening weekend.

	Plenty of moviegoers heeded that call. Imax accounted for $51.8 million (or 23%) of initial global ticket sales, standing as the company’s biggest opening in like-for-like markets — above a little blockbuster called “Avengers: Endgame.” And it’s currently pacing ahead of “Oppenheimer,” which was a juggernaut in Imax with $35 million worldwide to start and $180 million by the end of its run, contributing roughly 20% of overall revenues.

	Imax, which is offering “The Odyssey” (and “The Odyssey” alone) for three entire weeks, has been scheduling showtimes at odd hours of the day to accommodate the stratospheric demand. Movie lovers are going to great lengths to see the film in the coveted 70mm auditoriums, with some deranged determined fans booking 2 a.m. and 7 a.m. screenings of the nearly three-hour epic. Those 70mm screens — and there are only 41 in the entire world — contributed a mighty $6.3 million over the weekend. If you didn’t snag a ticket a year ago, good luck — many Imax locations are fully booked through August.

	“We have showtimes in certain formats that are sold out for next weekend, the weekend after, and even mid-week,” says Universal’s president of domestic distribution Jim Orr. Of those brave souls who opted to watch “The Odyssey” in the dead of the night, lest they not get a chance to see the movie in 70mm at all, Orr jokes, “We thank them for their patronage and hope they drive home safely.”
	
		All Hail Nolan	

	Nolan has already been established as the rare director who can get people to buy a ticket on the strength of his name. As the follow-up to “Oppenheimer,” a somber period piece about the father of the atomic bomb, “The Odyssey” is further proof that Nolan can get butts in seats no matter the genre. It’s one thing to pack ’em in for comic book adaptations (“The Dark Knight” series), sci-fi mind-benders (“Inception” and “Interstellar”) or sweeping war dramas (“Dunkirk”). But who else could turn a sword-and-sandal adventure that’s R-rated, nearly three hours long and isn’t a sequel into populist fare?
	

	“By any right, this three-hour epic would be viewed as a risk if anyone else had done it,” says Shawn Robbins, director of movie analytics at Fandango and founder of Box Office Theory. “But he’s used his success to cultivate a wide audience. There’s an incredible amount of goodwill for Nolan as a filmmaker, not just in the industry but also with casual moviegoers.”

	After “The Odyssey,” studios might not even wait to hear Nolan’s next pitch before writing the filmmaker a blank check.
	
		All-Audience Blockbuster 	

	People have jokingly referred to “The Odyssey” as the ultimate dad movie, yet Nolan even captivated the TikTok generation. A surprising 50% of audience members were between 18 and 34 years old, underscoring the director’s appeal to cinephiles of all ages. That’s a notable turnout for moviegoers under a certain age, considering Bronze-Age epics aren’t exactly the genre du jour of Gen Z.

	“The easy and natural assumption is ‘The Odyssey’ would have an older fanbase,” says Robbins. “But Nolan tapped into all crowds. It’s a sword-and-sandal movie, but it’s also a fantasy adventure and a reflective character study. The story translates across ages.”

	Contrast that to Steven Spielberg’s sci-fi conspiracy thriller “Disclosure Day,” which appealed overwhelmingly to Gen Xers and Baby Boomers, with 60% of opening weekend crowds above 35 years old. Spielberg, a household name thanks to behemoths like “Jaws,” “E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial” and “Jurassic Park,” may be the most commercially successful director of all time. After spending much of the past decade on adult dramas like “The Post,” “The Fabelmans” and “West Side Story,” however, he’s not as popular in the eyes of younger moviegoers as he is with their parents.
	
		All About the Big Screen	

	Any ol’ marketing guru can turn a movie like “Barbie,” “Wicked” or “The Devil Wears Prada 2” into a leave-the-home worthy event. With “The Odyssey,” Universal turned not just the film but the cinematic experience into a cultural happening. People weren’t just eager to see “The Odyssey,” they wanted to watch the battles with gods, monsters and witches on the biggest of screens. That was all by design. “The Odyssey’s” big-screen journey began long, long ago — last summer, when Imax tickets went on sale (and promptly sold out) an entire year before the movie’s premiere. To further drive home the point that Nolan’s films are designed for cinemas, audiences were treated last December to the nearly six-minute prologue of “The Odyssey” ahead of Imax 70mm screenings of “Sinners” and “One Battle After Another.” There was no confusion that theaters would be the only place to catch “The Odyssey” for the foreseeable future.
	

	“Nolan is one of the most trusted filmmakers ever, and audiences know he’s going to deliver something that you want to experience on the big screen,” Orr says.
	
		All the Hype Was Justified	

	Anticipation was high for “The Odyssey” on the heels of 2023’s “Oppenheimer,” Nolan’s first Oscar best-picture winner. But goodwill wouldn’t last long without quality to match the hype. In that respect, “The Odyssey” doesn’t just talk the talk. It’s one of the best-reviewed films of the year, securing an “A” grade on CinemaScore exit polls and a sterling 95% on Rotten Tomatoes. Word-of-mouth is everything in today’s fractured moviegoing landscape, and this weekend’s sentiment was clear: If you didn’t see “The Odyssey,” you were better left behind on the sandy beaches of Troy.

schema.headline: 5 Reasons ‘The Odyssey’ Defied Box Office Expectations, Including Imax’s Monster $52 Million Start

title: World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup once again demonstrated the value of home-team interest to broadcasters, with television audiences across Europe skyrocketin
body:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup once again demonstrated the value of home-team interest to broadcasters, with television audiences across Europe skyrocketing when national teams were still alive in the tournament before declining after their elimination. Yet, fans still turned up in force for the July 19 final opposing Spain to Argentina, proving that while national pride drives the biggest spikes, a good World Cup can stand on its own as not just a powerful live television event, but a cultural one as well.
	

	The match attracted a raft of stars, including Justin Bieber, who performed at halftime, Beyoncé and Jay-Z, Rihanna and A$AP Rocky, Timothée Chalamet with his father Marc and girlfriend Kylie Jenner, Tom Cruise, Javier Bardem and Penelope Cruz, David and Victoria Beckham, Serena Williams, Tom Brady, Carlos Alcaraz, Ronaldo and Ronaldinho, underscoring the World Cup’s reach beyond sport.


	Spain, which won the World Cup 1-0, unsurprisingly delivered the tournament’s largest audience ever recorded in the country and the second-most-watched TV event in Spanish history, surpassed only by the penalty shootout in Spain’s Euro 2012 semi-final against Portugal.

	As Spaniards around the country sat in anguish at Spain’s seeming inability to convert its slew of scoring chances in goals, the final averaged 15.7 million viewers and an extraordinary 87.1% audience share across RTVE’s La 1, regional broadcaster 3Cat, La 2 and DAZN, reaching a cumulative 20.1 million people. 

	Spain’s media have been quick to draw conclusion to the triumph. “It’s not difficult to see in this team, in the diversity of its players’ origins, in the character of its stars and its style of play and ability to connect with fans and citizens across-the-board a reflection of what this country is and can be in the 21st century,” said editorial on Monday in Spanish newspaper El País.
	

	The contrast with the beaten semi-finalists was telling. France’s audience took the steepest hit once ‘Les Bleus’ were knocked out. France’s semi-final against Spain delivered the country’s biggest television audience of the year with 20.2 million viewers and a 73% audience share on M6, according to Mediametrie; but by the time the final rolled around, the audience had fallen to 12.24 million with a 59.2% share. That’s roughly 8 million fewer viewers and a loss of almost 14 audience-share points once French hopes had ended.

	The U.K. proved slightly less fickle. England’s semi-final defeat to Argentina averaged 22.1 million viewers and peaked at 24 million on BBC One, making it the UK’s biggest television broadcast of the year so far. 

	The final, despite England getting beaten, still averaged 13.9 million viewers, peaked at 15.8 million, and commanded a 64% audience share across BBC One and iPlayer. ITV viewership reportedly peaked at 3.4 million. The drop-off was modest compared with France’s, suggesting British viewers were a little more willing to stick around once their own team had exited.

	In Italy, where the home team failed to even qualify for the tournament, audiences flocked to the Spain-Argentina final. The match dominated in linear TV prime time on state broadcaster RAI’s RAI-1 channel with roughly 12.5 million viewers and an average 65% share that rose to 74% during the extra time. 

	In the U.S., the tournament’s strong performance on Fox has already stirred talk of the next fight over FIFA’s media rights, with reports suggesting streamers like Netflix, YouTube and Apple could enter the mix. ESPN chairman Jimmy Pitaro said at a Fanatics Fest conference in New York that Disney-owned ESPN is interested in bidding for U.S. broadcast rights to the next World Cup.Fox paid $485 million for the rights to the 2026 tournament, and together with Spanish-language partner Telemundo is expected to pull in roughly $850 million in advertising revenue — with Fox alone reportedly earning as much as $250 million from hydration-break commercials, according to SportsPro.

schema.headline: World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses

title: Lewis Pullman Joins Julia Garner in Apple TV Thriller ‘Guilty Creatures’
description:
Lewis Pullman is set to star opposite Julia Garner in the upcoming Apple TV series “Guilty Creatures,” Variety has learned. 

	The show, with Garner
body:
Lewis Pullman is set to star opposite Julia Garner in the upcoming Apple TV series “Guilty Creatures,” Variety has learned. 

	The show, with Garner as a lead and executive producer, was announced on July 6. It is an adaptation of the Mikita Brottman true crime book “Guilty Creatures: Sex, God, and Murder in Tallahassee, Florida.” 

	The logline for the show states that the series, “delves into the psyche of a torrid romance and subsequent act of murder between two young, adulterous but God-fearing lovers, unraveling their complex lives and the emotional toll of living as killers for 18 years.” 
	

	This is Pullman’s second “Creature” based title in recent times. He just starred in the Netflix original film “Remarkably Bright Creatures,” which was itself based on the book of the same name by Shelby Van Pelt. Pullman is also known for his work in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, starring as Sentry in “Thunderbolts.” He will reprise that tole in “Avengers: Doomsday.” His other credits include the Apple series “Lessons in Chemistry,” Amazon’s “Outer Range,” and films like “Top Gun: Maverick” and “Salem’s Lot.” He will be seen next year in “Spaceballs: The New One,” starring alongside his father, Bill Pullman. 

	Pullman is repped by Anonymous Content, CAA & ID.

	Tomorrow Studios, an ITV Studios partner, developed the project and brought it to Apple. Craig Gillespie is attached to direct and executive produce under his Fortunate Jack Productions banner. Sarah DeLappe will write and executive produce. Stuart Zicherman will serve as showrunner and executive producer. Garner will executive produce through Alma Margo in addition to starring. Marty Adelstein, Becky Clements and Alissa Bachner will executive produce for Tomorrow Studios along with Annie Marter of Fortunate Jack and Brottman. Both Fortunate Jack and Alma Margo are under first-look deals with Tomorrow Studios.

schema.headline: Lewis Pullman Joins Julia Garner in Apple TV Thriller ‘Guilty Creatures’

title: ‘Afterlife With Archie’ Series From ‘Riverdale’ Team Greenlit at Disney+
description:
Disney+ has ordered the series version of the “Afterlife With Archie” comics, Variety has learned.

	The show was originally reported to be in devel
body:
Disney+ has ordered the series version of the “Afterlife With Archie” comics, Variety has learned.

	The show was originally reported to be in development at the streamer in 2025 with a script-to-series commitment. Disney is slating the series for a potential launch around Halloween 2027. 

	As previously reported, the show is based on the comic book series from Roberto Aguirre-Sacasa and Francesco Francavilla. The logline states, “When a supernatural spell from a certain teen-witch backfires, unleashing an army of the possessed dead upon Riverdale, Archie and his friends must fight to stay alive, testing the friendships, romances, and loyalties that have always held them together.”
	

	Aguirre-Sacasa wrote the script and serves as executive producer via Muckle Man Productions. Greg Berlanti, Sarah Schechter, and Leigh London Redman of Berlanti Productions also executive produce, along with Jon Goldwater of Archie Comics Studios and Jimmy Gibbons of Muckle Man. Warner Bros. Television, where both Berlanti Productions and Muckle Man are under overall deals, is the studio. 

	“‘Afterlife With Archie’ has always been about more than just zombies — it’s about the friendships, loyalty and humanity that make Riverdale worth fighting for,” said Ayo Davis, president of Disney Kids & Family. “Roberto, Greg, Sarah and Jon have such a deep appreciation for these characters, and they’ve built something that’s equal parts thrilling and heartfelt. Fans are going to fall hard for this new version of the gang … and maybe scream a little too.”

	Aguirre-Sacasa is repped by WME and Sloane Offer. Berlanti and Schechter are repped by CAA, as is  Goldwater.
	

	Aguirre-Sacasa and Berlanti previously collaborated on the series “Riverdale,” which served as a dark reimagining of the Archie comics rife with murder, mystery, and jingle jangle. The show aired for seven seasons on The CW and remained highly popular throughout its run.

	“We are so happy to reunite with Roberto on this exciting series that takes us back into the Riverdale world that we so love, and we are thankful to Disney+ and WBTV for giving us the opportunity to revisit some of our favorite characters in a unique and thrilling way,” Berlanti and Schechter added. “A special thank you to Jon Goldwater for letting us continue to play within the beloved ‘Archie’ universe and trusting us to bring Archie’s various stories to life.”

	In addition to his work on “Riverdale,” Aguirre-Sacasa serves as chief creative officer for Archie Comics. He and Robert Hack also created the comics “Chilling Adventures of Sabrina,” which Aguirre-Sacasa developed into a Netflix series that aired for two seasons. He and Michael Grassi developed the “Riverdale” spinoff Katy Keene as well. Berlanti Productions and WBTV produced all of the shows.

	“The world of ‘Archie’ and Riverdale is the gift that keeps on giving. To bring Roberto Aguirre-Sacasa’s incredible story to life with our partners at Berlanti Productions, Archie Comics and Disney+ is a thrill for all of us at WBTVG,” said Channing Dungey, chairman and CEO of Warner Bros. Television Group and WBD US Networks.

	“This was the comic book that started it all. Before there was a ‘Riverdale,’ before there was a ‘Chilling Adventures of Sabrina,’ there was ‘Afterlife With Archie,’ which I collaborated on with the best horror artist in the business, Francesco Francavilla,” said Aguirre-Sacasa. “Getting to turn ‘Afterlife’ into a TV series for the amazing team at Disney+ with our incredible partners at Berlanti Productions, WB and, of course, Jon (Goldwater) and his ‘Archie’ family is truly a full-circle moment — and the ultimate dream project for me. Time to say a prayer for Archie, Betty, Veronica and the whole gang, ‘cause THE ZOMBIES ARE COMING!!!!”

	“I am absolutely thrilled to see the iconic ‘Afterlife With Archie’ come to life; this is a series that has meant so much to me since the first comic issue came out years ago,” added Goldwater. “This was a series always destined for live-action and finally getting the chance to make that happen is like a dream come true. Not only that, getting back in the saddle to work once more with Roberto, Greg and Sarah truly feels like a homecoming. I’m so grateful to everyone at Disney+ for giving this series a chance and cannot wait for the zombie invasion to take over TV screens worldwide.”

schema.headline: ‘Afterlife With Archie’ Series From ‘Riverdale’ Team Greenlit at Disney+

title: Jalen Brunson Talks Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Giving Back and Whether the Knicks Will Visit the Trump White House
description: Jalen Brunson isn’t sure yet if his New York Knicks will visit the Trump White House after winning the NBA championship and thought it was “really c
body:
Jalen Brunson isn’t sure yet if his New York Knicks will visit the Trump White House after winning the NBA championship and thought it was “really cool” that Taylor Swift was cheering on the team courtside at Madison Square Garden during Game 4 of the NBA Finals. As for Swift’s star-studded wedding at MSG and the rumors that the Knicks’ starting five were invited? He found out the same way the rest of the world did (online) but feels his team’s play may have influenced her choice of wedding venue.
	

	The King of New York revealed all this during a conversation last week at Variety’s inaugural Sports & Entertainment Studio in New York, which included talks with Super Bowl-winning quarterback Russell Wilson; NASCAR legend Jimmie Johnson; NCAA champion and ESPN analyst Jay Williams; Kristin Lemkau, CEO of J.P. Morgan Wealth Management; Kristyn Cook, EVP at State Farm; and Carolyn Braff, VP of Sports and Entertainment at PepsiCo.

	When asked whether, as media reports indicated, he and the Knicks’ starting five got the invite to the Taylor Swift/Travis Kelce wedding at MSG, Brunson offers, “It’s a great question. I found out the same way the rest of the world did. So, I don’t know if it was true or not, but I did not attend.”

	He does think, however, think the team’s historic NBA championship run, earning the Knicks its first title in 53 years and rousing the entire city, could have played a part in Swift and Kelce getting married at the place dubbed the “World’s Most Famous Arena.” “Maybe. Maybe,” he replies. “But knowing how weddings work, I feel like that may have been predetermined a while back, but especially knowing how tightly scheduled the Garden is, I don’t know. But it was definitely, probably, clearly a memorable night for them.”
	

	Swift, meanwhile, received some blowback from Knicks’ fans for cheering the team on courtside at Game 4 of the NBA Finals at MSG, even though she’d recently appeared courtside with her then-fiancé Kelce, an Ohio native who was rooting for the opposing Cleveland Cavaliers against the Knicks. But Brunson enjoyed having her root for his team. “Yeah, it’s cool. It’s really cool having her there,” he maintains. “I feel like her being in Cleveland for courtside one game, obviously, I think Travis and the Kelces are from Ohio, so I get that. And she’s been at the Knicks’ games before. So, I mean, whoever comes, as long as you’re cheering us on, we appreciate it. And I think that was probably a really good game to come to as well.”

	As for whether the Knicks will visit the Trump White House, as their owner James Dolan indicated, Brunson can’t say for now. “That’s something that we actually haven’t discussed as a team. And so obviously, people are making assumptions and saying everything, but together we haven’t discussed it yet. And so we’ll cross our bridge when we get to it.”

	When Brunson was informed that if they do visit, they’d be the first NBA team to visit the Trump White House, he replied, “Interesting… Interesting.”

	Brunson was joined by Kristin Lemkau, who not only serves as the CEO of J.P. Morgan Wealth Management but also recruited the New York Knicks star to the JPMorganChase Athlete Council, a “collection of nine athletes who will meet every few months to reflect on their own experiences and inform how JPMC approaches its athlete clients,” with a focus on providing information and resources to younger athletes on how to manage their money. Dwyane Wade is the chair of the Athlete Council, and is joined by Brunson, Tom Brady, Sue Bird, Ally Love, Alex Morgan, Megan Rapinoe, Kayvon Thibodeaux and A’ja Wilson. “We observed an emerging problem of athletes having a unique set of financial circumstances, which is their careers are often short-lived,” explains Lemkau. “Three years, I think, is about the average professional career. Only 2% of college athletes will ever make it to the pros. And then when their career ends, they need to find a second career and they’re often not as well-equipped to do that or they didn’t actually get a college degree because they went pro right away. And one of the things I’m really proud of in my business, we can offer advice to anyone. We can take somebody who has $5 or Jalen Brunson. And so, I think there’s a responsibility, particularly as this money is going to kids younger and younger and younger, to get them to think about longevity.”
	



	

									Getty Images
	



	Brunson agrees, adding, “Once you start making money, everyone thinks that you’re going to be making this type of money for the rest of your life. And it’s not the case. Like she said, you’re making this money for a very short amount of time. A lot of people have very short careers, and you don’t really end on your own accord… But to be able to have the mindset early of knowing that, ‘Hey… this income isn’t going to last for my entire career or even beyond my career, so how can I be resourceful and start to be smart with my money at an early age?’ It’ll help.”

	Following the Knicks’ championship win, in which Brunson was named NBA Finals MVP and scored 45 points in the close-out game, he had surgery to repair a tendon in his left wrist. Fans — including this writer — were surprised that Brunson turned in a Michael Jordan-esque performance in the Finals with a bum wrist, and he sported a cast to the interview. “I was more in pain when I wasn’t in the game versus when we were actually playing. I feel like the adrenaline took over. But yeah, it was definitely an obstacle, but we found a way through,” he describes, adding, “Because it’s my tendon and my structure, my wrist is fine. There was soft tissue that was holding my tendon and it kept flipping and snapping over the bone every time I shot, every time I flicked my wrist, and so clearly it was causing pain, causing friction… I’ll be in this [cast] for about another six weeks, and then from that point on, it’s just my rehab after that. But I should be good.”

	Brunson co-hosts the popular Playmaker HQ podcast “The Roommates Show” with his Knicks and Villanova teammate (and good pal) Josh Hart, and says he will be “starting a media company.” He’s also keen to cameo on his favorite show, “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit,” starring his favorite fan, Mariska Hargitay.

	“I would love to,” he says of an “SVU” cameo. “Mariska has become a great friend of mine. She was an idol. She was someone I looked up to. I think obviously I feel like everyone who watches this show feels like she’s just a role model for all. And so, she’s still that for me, but then obviously getting to know her, know her family — our family is intertwining. It’s been a great friendship and she’s been very supportive of me and I try to be very supportive of her as well.”
	

	The Knicks’ championship win has caused a flurry of NBA free agency activity, with a number of rival teams adding major pieces — Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat, Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers — to challenge them for the throne. Even though the Knicks lost backup center (and defensive maestro) Mitchell Robinson to the Boston Celtics, Brunson still believes that his team has what it takes to run it back. “Oh, I still have confidence. I will always have confidence in myself and my team,” he declares. “Not because we won, because I know the type of character guys that we have. I know how hard my team works. I know how hard I work. I know what our mindset is. Our mindset is just to get better every single day. And so even though, yes, we did win this past year, going forward that literally means nothing.”

schema.headline: Jalen Brunson Talks Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Giving Back and Whether the Knicks Will Visit the Trump White House

title: Ariana Grande's BF Ricky Alvarez Dances With Brother Frankie at Brooklyn Show
description:
Ariana Grande
                          My Man and Bro Are Dancing Together!!
body:
Ariana Grande
                          My Man and Bro Are Dancing Together!!
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 20, 2026
                            7:41 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Ariana Grande's rekindled romance with Ricky Alvarez is getting a big thumbs-up from her brother Frankie ... or, in Ricky's case, a few pats on the head.
      
    
            Ricky joined Frankie in the crowd Sunday night at Barclays Center in Brooklyn for Ariana's "Eternal Sunshine Tour" ... where the two danced together as Ari performed "Warm."
      
    
      
      FRANKIE PATTING RICKY’S HEAD DURING WARM 😭😭😭😭 pic.twitter.com/R841l8xdNM
              @chaescvnty
          
  
    
            Check out the video ... Frankie gets especially affectionate at one point, reaching over and repeatedly patting Ricky's head while they groove to the music.
      

            It was Ricky's second straight night supporting his girlfriend at Barclays.
      
    
            As TMZ previously reported ... Ricky was spotted during Saturday's show singing and dancing along to "Imperfect for You."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Ariana and Ricky have been inseparable lately after rekindling the romance they first started back in 2015 ... though we've been told they're taking things slow.
      
    
            We've seen Ariana and Ricky pack on the PDA in the Big Apple ... with Ricky grabbing a handful of Ariana's backside before a romantic stroll through Central Park.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Ari's even been changing her old Ricky lyrics in "Thank U, Next" to give him a kinder review ... and judging by Frankie's reaction, he's clearly warming up to this romantic remix too.

title: President Trump Shares First Pic of J.D. Vance's Newborn
description:
President Donald Trump
                          Picture This ... I've Got First Look At J.D.'s N
body:
President Donald Trump
                          Picture This ... I've Got First Look At J.D.'s Newborn
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 20, 2026
                            6:58 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            J.D. Vance and wife Usha Vance welcomed their fourth child over the weekend ... and naturally, Donald Trump made the moment about himself ... sharing the first photo of the newborn.
      
    
            Trump gave the world the first look at baby Alec Neel ... posting the newborn on his Truth Social account and calling the kid "a perfect baby boy."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            POTUS is the first one to share a photo of baby Alec ... not his parents.
      
    
            Neither the Vice President nor Second Lady is in the pic ... it's just Alec and two of his older siblings sitting on some furniture in a hospital room.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Usha gave birth at Walter Reed Medical Center in Maryland on Sunday ... at least J.D. was able to break that news, rather than his boss.
      
    
            J.D. and Usha have been married since 2014 ... and their other children include 9-year-old Ewan, 6-year-old Vivek, and 4-year-old Mirabel.
      
    
            Maybe when Alec grows up, he will brag about Trump being the first one to share his photo. 🤷

title: Nolan Wells' Family and Friends Gather For Funeral
description:
Nolan Wells Funeral
                          Watch Loved Ones Say Their Final Goodbyes
body:
Nolan Wells Funeral
                          Watch Loved Ones Say Their Final Goodbyes
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 20, 2026
                            9:12 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 20, 2026
                                10:27 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
            Nolan Wells' loved ones gathered to say their final goodbyes Monday ... honoring the 18-year-old whose life ended far too soon.
      
    
            The funeral service is happened at Center Pointe Church in Ocean Springs, Mississippi, where Rev. Al Sharpton officiated and family attorney Ben Crump delivered a call for justice.
      
    
            Sharpton announced during his remarks that he'll be offering a $50,000 reward to anyone who comes forward with information about Nolan's death ... and he said Tyler Perry will match that amount.
      
    
            As we reported ... Nolan disappeared after traveling by boat with friends to Horn Island for a Fourth of July gathering. His friends returned to the mainland without him, and his body was found two days later on Horn Island.
      

            Nolan's friends told investigators Nolan chose to stay behind because he believed he could catch another ride -- an account his parents have publicly questioned.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            Crump is conducting an independent investigation, while official autopsy and toxicology results remain pending. The findings are expected to be presented to a grand jury.
      
    
            Colin Kaepernick paid for an independent autopsy ... and Perry paid for the funeral.

title: Jessie J Reveals She and Chanan Colman Split 'Some Time Ago'
description:
Jessie J
                          Quietly Split from Chanan Colman
body:
Jessie J
                          Quietly Split from Chanan Colman
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 20, 2026
                            9:39 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Jessie J and Chanan Colman are broken up ... and apparently have been for some time ... according to the singer.
      
    
            The Grammy nominee announced on social media Monday that she and the basketball player split "some time ago " ... with Jessie calling it a "sad and difficult situation."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            It's unclear how long they've been broken up, but Jessie says their priority has been their 3-year-old son, Sky. She says they're working on being the best co-parents they can be.
      

            In announcing the split, Jessie shared a sweet snap of the two of them with their son, wearing personalized jackets that say "Mommy," "Daddy," and "Sky."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            The "Price Tag" singer wrote in the caption ... "Unfortunately being in the public eye this is the easiest way to let people know the truth, before rumours circulate or a false narrative is written."
      
    
            Jessie also explained that they still love and support each other as friends, adding ... "So if you see us out and about together as a family, or posting each other on socials every now and again. That's exactly what we are doing."
      
    
            She says they plan to raise and love Sky together "with a positive friendship."
      
    
            Jessie and Chanan had been together for about 5 years. They welcomed Sky back in May 2023 and the sports star was by her side during her scary breast cancer battle last year.

title: 'RHOSLC' Angie Katsanevas and Shawn Trujillo Separate After 27 Years of Marriage
description:
'Real Housewives of Salt Lake City'
                          Splitsville For Angie & Shawn
body:
'Real Housewives of Salt Lake City'
                          Splitsville For Angie & Shawn
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 20, 2026
                            9:04 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 20, 2026
                                9:40 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            "The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City" stars Angie Katsanevas and Shawn Trujillo are throwing in the towel on their marriage.
      
    
            According to Page Six, multiple sources say Angie and Shawn have separated after 27 years as husband and wife.
      
    
            Some social media accounts are claiming infidelity is to blame, but TMZ has learned that's NOT true. A source with direct knowledge tells us ... reports that there was an affair have no merit.
      
    
            It's unclear when exactly Angie and Shawn separated, but it's worth noting Angie didn't celebrate their anniversary on Instagram last month.
      

            Contrast that with 2025 ... when Angie had A LOT to say about their anniversary.
      
    
        Waiting for your permission to load the Instagram Media.
      
    
            She wrote at the time ... "A Hair Affair #26YEARS. Seems like yesterday I booked an appointment for a blowdry with this dreamy guy I saw on the cover of a romance novel in the grocery store checkout. Long hair like Fabio, big muscles, and known to be lethal with scissors. The rest is HAIRstory."
      
    
            Season 7 of 'RHOSLC' taped earlier this year -- production wrapped in May -- so it'll be interesting to see if the couple's split will play out on camera.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            During the show's fourth season, Angie had to deal with rumors that Shawn was cheating on her with multiple men.
      
    
            And, not too long ago, people noticed Shawn started following divorce coaches on IG. Shawn told Reality Shrine it didn't have anything to do with his relationship with Angie ... claiming he was simply trying to help out a loved one going through a tough time.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Angie and Shawn tied the knot back in 1999.
      
    
            They have a 15-year-old daughter, Elektra.
